description: Phishing templates impersonating package delivery and shipping companies

templates.name: DHL Package Delivery
suggested_subject_lines: DHL: Your shipment is on its way
Shipment notification: Package awaiting delivery confirmation
DHL Express: Delivery attempted — reschedule required
templates.notes: Highest click-rate category. Nearly universal — works across all employee levels.

templates.name: Package Pickup Notice
suggested_subject_lines: Package ready for pickup — confirm your details
Delivery: Your package is waiting at the facility
Final notice: Package will be returned if not claimed
templates.notes: A reliable beginner-level lure with broad appeal — nearly everyone is expecting a package. Click rates peak during holiday shopping seasons. Keep the sender generic, since most targets won't scrutinize which carrier it claims to be.

templates.name: Holiday Package Delivery Hold
suggested_subject_lines: Delivery Exception: Package held at distribution center due to unpaid customs fee
Shipment Notification: Address verification required for holiday delivery
Courier Alert: Action needed to deliver your holiday package
templates.notes: Peak holiday shopping seasons (Q4) bring massive increases in home and office deliveries. Users are highly prone to clicking delivery alerts to ensure gifts arrive on time.
